Rx
San Francisco DPH · BHS

SUS New Admit Workflow Guides

Substance Use Services
1

Residential Admission & Consent Verification

Formally admit the member and verify legal compliance before clinical documentation begins
Admin or Clinical Staff
🛑 Context Requirement
You must be logged into a department whose name ends in "RESIDENTIAL". Outpatient departments do not support the inpatient admission workflow. Verify your department label in the Epic toolbar before proceeding.
1
System Access
Log In & Verify Department Context
Launch PRD Hyperspace via the Citrix Gateway. Before doing anything else, confirm the department shown in your Epic toolbar ends in "RESIDENTIAL." If it does not, switch departments now.
Department must end in "RESIDENTIAL" ✱ PRD Hyperspace via Citrix Gateway
2
Navigation
Search Patient Station → New Admission
Navigate to Patient Station — do not use Patient Lookup, which is for outpatient context. Search for the member and select New Admission.
⚠ Do Not Use Patient Lookup
Patient Lookup will not surface the inpatient admission workflow. Always start from Patient Station for residential admits.
Patient Station› Search Member› New Admission
3
Data Entry
Complete All Admission Fields
Fill every required field in the Admission Navigator. Missing any field will prevent the Admit button from activating.
Patient Encounter Creation — Expected Date, Unit, Patient Class (SUS Res), Destination tab, Room/Bed assignment, Checklist, and Admit button
a
Enter Expected Date, Unit, and Admitting Provider
b
Set Patient Class to SUS Res — wrong class causes downstream billing and reporting errors
c
Open the Destination tab → assign a specific Room and Bed
d
Review the administrative Checklist for any missing data → click Admit
🛑 Hard Stop — Patient Class
Patient Class must be set to "SUS Res". Any other class will route the encounter incorrectly and affect authorization eligibility.
4
Compliance — Consent Verification
Chart Review → BHS SnapShot → Verify Consents
After the admission is created, navigate to Chart Review > BHS SnapShot and verify all three required consent forms are present and current.
Chart Review› BHS SnapShot› Consent Status
Required Form 1
Treatment Terms
Consent to treat / program rules
Required Form 2
HIPAA
Notice of Privacy Practices
Required Form 3
Care Everywhere
Epic network data sharing
⚠ Rule — Missing or Expiring Consents
If any of the three forms are missing or expire within 30 days, obtain new signatures before proceeding. Upload completed paper forms via Media Manager. Do not advance to clinical documentation until all three are current. See the Consent Workflow tab for the full digital consent process.
✓ Outcome — Workflow 1 Complete
Member is admitted to the residential program. All required consents are on file. Proceed to Workflow 2: Initial Clinical Assessment.
2

Initial Clinical Assessment

OP / IOP / OTP — Complete flowsheets, assessment note, and LPHA documentation before submitting authorization
AOD Counselor + LPHA
Role key: AOD Counselor LPHA Only
Rooming & Consent
Data Entry
Documentation
Finalize
Phase A — Rooming & Consent
A1
Prerequisite
Verify Consents in BHS Snapshot
Before rooming begins, open the BHS Snapshot and confirm all required consent forms are on file. Missing forms must be resolved before proceeding to clinical documentation.
BHS Snapshot Consent to Treat ROI / 42 CFR Part 2 HIPAA Notice (if missing → Consent Workflow tab)
A2
Rooming Activity
Add / Link BH Episode in Rooming
In the Rooming Navigator, locate the BH Episode activity. Either link an existing open episode or create a new one for this visit.
⚠ Important
The BH Episode must be active and linked before data entry. An unlinked visit will not route charges or assessments correctly.
Rooming → BH Episodes tab — link or create a BH Episode before data entry
Phase B — Data Entry (SUS Assessments Tab)
B1
Navigate
Open SUS Assessments Tab
Within the patient chart, navigate to the SUS Assessments tab.
Patient Chart› SUS Assessments Tab
B2
Primary Flowsheet
SUD LOC Flowsheet — Adult or Youth
Open the appropriate SUD Level of Care flowsheet. Select the correct Assessment Type from the dropdown at the top before entering any data.
Assessment Type: "OP/IOP/OTP – Initial" ✱ Adult LOC Flowsheet Youth LOC Flowsheet (if patient <18)
B3
Addendum Flowsheets — Complete Within This Phase
Triage Guide & Timely Access Flowsheets
While still in data entry, complete both addendum flowsheets before moving to documentation. These are part of the data-entry phase, not post-note addenda.

🗂 Triage Guide Flowsheet

  • Urgency / acuity screening
  • Safety risk indicators
  • Preferred language / access needs

⏱ Timely Access Flowsheet

  • Date/time of first contact
  • Date/time of assessment
  • Access compliance verification
⚠ Sequencing Rule
Both Triage Guide and Timely Access must be completed before moving to documentation. They cannot be added retroactively as post-note addenda.
Screenings tab — SUS Adult Triage Guide and Timely Access flowsheets
Phase C — Documentation (Assessment Docs)
C1
Navigate
Assessment Docs → Create New Note
Navigate to Assessment Docs within the SUS tab. Click + New Note and select the correct note type.
Note Type: "BHS SUS Assessment" ✱
SUS Assessments› Assessment Docs› + New Note›
SUS Assessments tab → SUD LOC Adult flowsheet and Assessment Docs — BHS SUS Assessment note pulls LOC data
BHS SUS Assessment
C2
LPHA-Only Steps LPHA Only
Diagnostic Justification & Problem List Update
LPHAs must complete two additional tasks before signing the assessment note.
Task A
Diagnostic Justification
Complete the Diagnostic Justification note with DSM-5 diagnosis and clinical rationale for the SUD LOC. Required before authorization submission.
Task B
Update Problem List
Add active SUD diagnosis codes to the Problem List and link to the current encounter. Resolve any outdated problems.
BHS SUS Assessment note — automatically pulls SUD LOC Assessment data into the note
Phase D — Finalize (Wrap-Up)
D1
Billing
Add Visit Charges
In the Wrap-Up activity, add the appropriate visit charge code for the initial assessment encounter.
D2
State Reporting
Document BHS Direct Service Time
Record total direct service time in the BHS Direct Service Time flowsheet within Wrap-Up. Required for state reporting.
🛑 Do Not Skip
BHS Direct Service Time is mandatory for DPH reporting compliance. Cannot be back-filled after the encounter is closed.
Wrap-Up — BHS Direct Service Time (Individual) and Charge Capture sections
✓ Outcome — Workflow 2 Complete
Assessment documented. LPHA tasks signed. Proceed to Workflow 3: Authorization Linkage & Bed Days to submit the REF283 and complete account setup.
3

Authorization Linkage & Initial Account Setup

Submit REF283 · Link UM-approved authorization to the hospital account · Add bed days for billing
LPHA + Census / Account Staff
✓ WF1 Done
Admitted
✓ WF2 Done
Assessment Complete
← WF3 — YOU ARE HERE
Submit REF283 · LOC Assessment · Link Auth · Add Bed Days · Assign to Account
1
Authorization Submission LPHA
Submit Referral Order — REF283
Place the authorization referral order. Complete all three sections before signing.
Orders Tab› + New› After Visit ◉›
Orders tab → Sidebar Summary → Orders → + New — showing existing New Discharge Orders section with REF283
Referrals → Residential
🔍 Order Name
Referral to BHS UM - SUS Residential Authorization
Order and Order Set Search — After Visit selected, Referrals → Residential → Referral to BHS UM - SUS Residential Authorization highlighted
Section 1
Program Info
▸ Dept Specialty: Substance Use Services
▸ Requestor name & contact
▸ Requestor license type/number
▸ Program Admission
Section 2
Level of Care
▸ Admission Date
▸ Est. Discharge Date
▸ Service: 3.1 3.3 3.5
▸ Type of Authorization
▸ Requested Days/Units
Section 3
Clinical Info
▸ Presenting diagnoses
▸ Reason for seeking services (precipitant, recent crises, co-occurring issues)
▸ Barriers to OP/IOP
▸ Capacity to participate
✓ Accept → ✓ Sign → Check Associate Diagnoses →
Orders screen — New Discharge Orders with REF283 listed, Sign button highlighted bottom right
✓ Accept
Associate Diagnoses — select the SUD diagnosis (e.g. Opioid abuse) and click Accept to link to the REF283 order
2
LOC Assessment AOD or LPHA
Complete & Finalize SUD LOC Adult Flowsheet
Under the Navigators tab, open and complete the SUD LOC Adult flowsheet. Work through all ASAM dimension fields. Select the correct Assessment Type before entering data.
Assessment Type: "OP/IOP/OTP – Initial" or "Residential – Initial" ✱
🛑 Crucial Step — Mark Complete & Close
After completing all fields, mark the assessment as "Complete" and click the close button. BHS UM cannot view the clinical justification until this is done. An "In Progress" status blocks authorization review.
Navigators Tab› SUS Assessments› SUD LOC Adult› Mark Complete› Close ✓
3
Documentation & Diagnosis LPHA
Generate Assessment Note & Enter Formal Diagnosis
Navigate to Assessment Docs → create a new note using type BHS SUS Assessment. The LPHA must also complete both tasks below before proceeding.
Assessment Docs Tab› + New Note› BHS SUS Assessment
Task A — LPHA
Diagnostic Justification
Clinical rationale for the DSM diagnosis and requested ASAM LOC. Required by BHS UM before auth can be approved.
Task B — LPHA
DSM Diagnosis → Problem List
Enter the formal DSM Substance Use Disorder diagnosis in the Problem List and link to the current encounter.
4
Wait for UM Determination
Monitor for Approval — 24 Business Hours
Do not proceed to account linkage until UM approves. Watch for either signal:

📬 In Basket Notification

  • Epic In Basket message to requestor
  • Sent by BHS UM on decision

📄 Decision Letter in Chart

  • Auth letter uploaded to member chart
  • Check Media Manager or Documents
5
Navigate to Linkage Tool
Patient Workqueue (WQF) → Auth/Cert Linkage
Open the Patient Workqueue (WQF). Locate the member in the HS Res Auth/Cert queue for your program. Double-click their row. On the Update Admission screen, click More ▾ → Auth/Cert Linkage.
Patient Workqueues (WQF)› HS Res Auth/Cert [Program]› Double-click member›
Update Admission screen — starting point after double-clicking member in WQF
More ▾ → Auth/Cert Linkage
6
Read Auth Details — Write These Down
Double-Click REF283 Entry → Open Questionnaire
In the Auth/Certs list, find the row where the Orders column shows REF283. Double-click it. Click Questionnaire in the left sidebar and manually record both values before clicking Cancel.
Auth/Cert List — locate row where Orders column shows REF283, double-click to open
✏ Write These Down Before Clicking Cancel
Auth/Cert Questionnaire — Level of Care (BHS SUS 3.1), # Residential Days Approved (30), 1st Review Decision (Approved)
Level of Care
e.g. BHS SUS 3.1
# of Residential Days Approved
e.g. 30 days
⚠ Important Sequence
Click Cancel (not Close/Save) after noting the values. Then reopen the Auth/Cert record by double-clicking it again before proceeding to Bed Days.
7
Set Initial Bed Days
Bed Days Tab → + Add Entry
In the reopened Auth/Cert Entry, click Bed Days in the left sidebar. Click + Add Entry and complete all four fields using the values recorded in Step 6.
Bed Days tab — + Add Entry button, showing empty Authorizations section ready for entry
Bed Day Entry — Required Fields
From Date = Admission Date ✱ Day Type = LOC from Step 6 (e.g. BHS SUS 3.1) ✱ Auth Status = Authorized ✱ Total Days = # Approved Days from Step 6 ✱
✓ Accept
Bed Day entry filled — From Date: 1/28/2026, To Date: 2/26/2026, Day Type: BHS SUS 3.1, Auth Status: Authorized, Total Days: 30
— saves the bed day entry
8
Finalize Account Link
Select Auth/Cert → Assign → Verify Hosp Acct
Return to the Auth/Cert List. Click once to highlight the REF283 entry. Click ✓ Assign. The Hosp Acct column will populate with a number — confirming the authorization is linked to the admission account and ready for billing.
Auth/Cert List — before Assign: Hosp Acct column is empty
After clicking Assign — Hosp Acct column populated with account number (500003246), confirming active link
Hosp Acct: [Account #] populated ← confirms active link
✓ Outcome — Workflow 3 Complete
REF283 submitted. Authorization linked to the residential admission. Bed days documented. Account ready for billing. Initial authorization setup is fully complete.
4

Primary Consent Workflow — Digital First

Always use the digital path inside the patient chart · Media Manager is for paper backup only
Primary Path — Always Use This
Digital Consent (In Chart)
1
Open Patient Chart
Navigate to any active patient encounter or chart.
2
Click "More" → Consents
In the chart toolbar, click More ▾ and select Consents.
3
Verify Existing Forms
Review status of all current forms: Signed, Pending, or Missing.
4
Add Missing Forms
Click ＋ Add Form (green "+" button). Search and select the needed form.
Treatment Terms HIPAA Care Everywhere 42 CFR Part 2 ROI
5
Client Signs via Signature Pad
Present the signature pad. Client signs digitally. Witness signature if required.
✓
File & Sign to Complete
Click File & Sign. Form status updates to "Signed."
Secondary / Backup Only
Media Manager (Paper Scanning)
⚠ Use Only for Physical Paper
Media Manager is only for scanning paper forms that cannot be obtained digitally. Do not use as the default path.
1
Add New Media
Open Media Manager → + Add New Media.
2
Select Document Type
Choose the correct document category (e.g., "Consent Form – Paper").
!
Select Encounter (Required)
Required linkage — document will not index without an encounter association.
3
Scan / Import → Index → Upload
Scan, complete indexing fields, click Upload.
✓
Document Filed
Visible in Media Manager. Note: will not appear in BHS SnapShot consent status.
42 CFR Part 2: SUD records require a signed ROI before disclosure to any external party — including other DPH programs. Standard HIPAA TPO exceptions do not apply. Never disclose without a valid ROI on file.
5

SUS Assessments Tab — Visual UI Map

Where to find each flowsheet within the SUS Assessments tab in Epic Hyperspace

This map mirrors the actual Navigators panel layout in Epic Hyperspace — left sidebar with grouped sections, main content area showing the active flowsheet.

Navigators panel — Admission tab showing SUS Assessments group: SUD LOC Adult (active), SUD LOC RA, BARC-10

Screenings
Discharge

Allergies

Health Care Agents
Belongings

Orders
CalOMS

Annual Update
Discharge
Admin Discharge
SUS Assessments
SUD LOC Adult
SUD LOC RA
BARC-10
Adult SUD Level of Care Assessment
Assessment
Type of Assessment
OP/IOP/OTP - Initial
OP/IOP/OTP - Update
Residential - Initial
Perinatal Residential - Initial
Perinatal Residential - Switch to regular DMC-ODS
Not Applicable/Incomplete
Why is the client seeking services today?
Dimension 1: Acute Intoxication and/or Withdrawal Potential

Has the client used any of the above substances within the last 72 hrs?
Yes

Yes
No
↓ Dimensions 2–6 continue below…
⚡ SUD LOC Adult — Active
Primary assessment flowsheet. Found under SUS Assessments in the left sidebar. Select Assessment Type before entering any data. Must be marked Complete and closed for BHS UM to view.
🏠 SUD LOC RA — Residential Auth
Listed directly below SUD LOC Adult in the sidebar. Used after REF283 approval. Records UM decision, approved LOC (BHS SUS 3.x), days approved, and review cycle.
📊 BARC-10
Brief Assessment of Recovery Capital — 10-item standardized screening tool. Listed third under SUS Assessments. Complete as indicated by program protocol.
📋 CalOMS — State Reporting (separate sidebar group)
Admission — Required at episode start. Demographic + substance use baseline data for DHCS reporting.
Annual Update — Required each year the client remains enrolled.
Discharge — Required at episode end. Treatment outcome + discharge destination.
Admin Discharge — Administrative closure record.
Assessment Completion Status
✅ Complete
All fields filled. Marked Complete and closed. Visible to BHS UM for authorization review.
⏳ In Progress
Partial data entered, not yet marked Complete. UM cannot view. Cannot submit auth.
🔴 Not Started
Flowsheet never opened. Blocks all downstream authorization steps.
6

Residential Reauthorization — Continued Stay

Justify ongoing residential placement at each authorization period · Submit via BHS UM Work Queue

📅 Select Request Level

  • 1st continued stay request (30-day)
  • 2nd continued stay request (60-day)
  • 3rd continued stay request (90-day)
  • 4th or Other — specify

🏥 Current ASAM LOC

  • 3.1 Clinically Managed Low Intensity
  • 3.3 Clinically Managed Population-Specific
  • 3.5 Clinically Managed High Intensity
For Each Checked Dimension — Complete All Four Items
# Dimension Severity (0–4) 30-Day Progress / Challenges Clinical Rationale for Continued Stay
I Acute Intoxication / Withdrawal 0=None · 4=Severe Withdrawal sx changes, MAT response, lab trends Ongoing withdrawal risk needing 24-hr monitoring?
II Biomedical Conditions 0=None · 4=Severe Medical stability, wound care, chronic conditions Unstabilized medical needs requiring residential?
III Emotional / Behavioral / Cognitive 0=None · 4=Severe Psychiatric stability, affect regulation, trauma Co-occurring MH destabilizing recovery?
IV Readiness to Change 0=None · 4=Severe Motivation, stage of change, engagement Ambivalence needing intensive motivational work?
V Relapse / Continued Use Potential 0=None · 4=Severe Coping skills, triggers, cravings High relapse risk without 24-hr structure?
VI Recovery / Living Environment 0=None · 4=Severe Housing stability, sober network, social support Unsafe environment requiring residential placement?
Summary & Plan — Required for Every Reauth
🛑 UM Will Not Approve Without Both
Every reauthorization must include both a clinical summary and a detailed discharge plan. Vague or missing discharge plans are the #1 reason UM returns requests.
Clinical Summary
Brief narrative of clinical rationale supporting continued residential stay at time of the request.
Detailed Discharge Plan
  • ▸ Next level of care identified
  • ▸ Referrals made (with dates)
  • ▸ Follow-up appointments confirmed
  • ▸ Warm handoffs documented
  • ▸ Supports for safe transition
7

Resident Discharge Workflow — Six-Stage Sequence

Follow in order — each stage has a dependency on the previous · Hard Discharge is irreversible
1. Pre-Discharge
2. Transition
3. Documentation
4. State Reporting
5. Op. Closure
6. Hard Discharge
1
Stage 1 — Pre-Discharge
Verify Daily Notes & Care Plan Are Signed
Review the clinical chart. All daily progress notes must be signed and the current Care Plan co-signed before initiating discharge.
🛑 Hard Stop
Unsigned notes or Care Plan block Hard Discharge. Resolve all signatures first.
All Daily Notes — Signed ✓ Care Plan — Co-Signed ✓ Outstanding Orders — Resolved ✓
2
Stage 2 — Transition Planning
Document Warm Handoffs & Follow-Up Appointments
Confirm and document all post-discharge arrangements. Each handoff should be documented as a communication in the chart.

🤝 Warm Handoffs

  • Receiving program contacted
  • Client introduced to next provider
  • Records transfer authorized (42 CFR)

📅 Appointments

  • OP SUD program intake date
  • PCP / MAT prescriber follow-up
  • MH continuation appointment
3
Stage 3 — Documentation
New Note: BHS SUS Discharge
Navigate to Assessment Docs → + New Note. Select note type BHS SUS Discharge.
4
Stage 4 — State Reporting
Complete CalOMS Discharge Flowsheet
Navigate to SUS Assessments Tab → CalOMS Discharge flowsheet. Complete all required DHCS fields.
🛑 DHCS Mandate
Cannot be back-filled after Hard Discharge. Missing data risks contract non-compliance.
5
Stage 5 — Operational Closure
Return Belongings & Close MAR
Complete all operational tasks before Hard Discharge.
Belongings returned — signed receipt MAR — Closed & medications reconciled Room/Bed — marked Available
6
Stage 6 — Administrative Hard Discharge
Hard Discharge — Closes the Admission Encounter
The final irreversible step. Closes the residential encounter, removes from census, locks clinical documentation, and triggers billing finalization.
⚠ Irreversible
Cannot be undone without IT/admin intervention. Confirm all five prior stages are complete before clicking.
✓ Outcome
Patient removed from census. Encounter closed. Bed available. Discharge paperwork available via Media Manager.
8

Discharge Plan vs. Discharge Summary

Two separate documents — different purposes, audiences, and timing
💡 Key Distinction
Confusing these two documents leads to incomplete authorizations and regulatory deficiencies. Both may be required for a complete discharge.
📋 Discharge Plan — Option A
Forward-looking · Aftercare-focused · Required for Reauth
Perspective
Forward-looking. Describes what happens after the client leaves.
Primary Use
Required for every Reauthorization. UM will not approve without it.
Core Content
  • ▸ Next level of care identified
  • ▸ Step-down program/facility named
  • ▸ Medication plan at discharge
  • ▸ Referrals made with dates
  • ▸ Follow-up appointments confirmed
  • ▸ Housing / transportation plan
Timing
Updated at each auth period and finalized at discharge
Epic Location
Reauth Order — Discharge Plan Section
🛑 Reauth Requirement
Missing or vague Discharge Plan = #1 reason UM returns reauth requests.
📄 Discharge Summary — Option B
Retrospective · Comprehensive · Clinical record
Perspective
Retrospective. Reviews the full residential stay from admit to discharge.
Primary Use
Clinical record, continuity of care. Not used for auth decisions.
Core Content
  • ▸ Presenting problem at admission
  • ▸ Treatment milestones achieved
  • ▸ Progress across ASAM dimensions
  • ▸ Medications during stay
  • ▸ Incidents / behavioral events
  • ▸ Reason for exit (planned/AMA)
Timing
Completed at discharge only
Epic Location
Assessment Docs → BHS SUS Discharge Note
⚠ Not a Substitute
Discharge Summary does NOT replace the Discharge Plan for reauthorization.
Quick Decision Table
SituationDischarge Plan?Discharge Summary?
Submitting a reauthorization to UM✅ Required❌ Not used for auth
Client leaving the program today✅ Finalize✅ Complete
Sending records to a new treatment program⚠ Include (aftercare plan)✅ Primary document
Quality review / audit requestOptional✅ Yes
42 CFR Part 2: Discharge Summaries containing SUD treatment information require a valid, signed ROI before release to any external party. Standard HIPAA TPO exceptions do not apply to 42 CFR Part 2 records.
